title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa

seo.title: How to Make Chilaquiles Red Salsa — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chilaquiles Red Salsa. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Chilaquiles Red Salsa
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Chilaquiles Red Salsa recipe with toppings
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Chilaquiles Red Salsa — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a vibrant and comforting Mexican breakfast, transforming humble fried tortilla chips into a culinary masterpiece. Bathed in a rich, spicy red salsa, these softened chips are often topped with cheese, crema, and a fried egg, creating a symphony of textures and flavors. It's the perfect way to start your day with a taste of authentic Mexico.

ingredients.notes: cut into quarters, or 8 oz good quality tortilla chips
or sour cream thinned with a little milk, for topping

instructions.title: Roast Vegetables for Salsa
instructions.text: Place the quartered Roma tomatoes, serrano peppers, and peeled garlic cloves on a baking sheet. Roast under a broiler or in a hot oven at 400F (200C) for 10-15 minutes, turning once, until slightly charred and softened. Alternatively, pan-roast them in a dry skillet over medium-high heat until charred.

instructions.title: Blend the Salsa
instructions.text: Transfer the roasted vegetables to a blender. Add the roughly chopped white onion, chicken broth, and a generous pinch of salt. Blend until smooth. If you prefer a chunkier salsa, blend for a shorter time. Taste and adjust salt as needed.

instructions.title: Simmer the Red Salsa
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a medium saucepan or pot over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended salsa into the hot oil. Be cautious as it may splatter. Bring the salsa to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and let it cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly and the flavors meld.

instructions.title: Prepare Tortilla Chips
instructions.text: If using fresh corn tortillas, heat about 1 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium-high heat. Fry the tortilla quarters in batches until golden and crisp, about 2-3 minutes per batch. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil. Season lightly with salt. If using store-bought chips, skip this step.

instructions.title: Combine Chips and Salsa
instructions.text: Once the salsa has simmered and the chips are ready, add the fried tortilla chips directly into the simmering salsa. Gently toss the chips with the salsa until they are evenly coated. Cook for 1-2 minutes, just until the chips begin to soften but still retain some texture. Do not overcook, or they will become mushy.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the Chilaquiles Red Salsa to individual plates. Garnish generously with crumbled queso fresco, a drizzle of Mexican crema, thinly sliced red onion, and fresh chopped cilantro. For a heartier breakfast, top each serving with a freshly fried egg. Serve hot and enjoy this authentic Mexican delight.

tips: For a deeper, smoky flavor, char the tomatoes and peppers well before blending. You can even add a dried chile de arbol or guajillo to the roasting process for extra heat and complexity.
The key to perfect chilaquiles is timing. Add the chips to the salsa just before serving to prevent them from becoming too soggy. You want them softened, but not mushy.
Don't be afraid to customize your toppings! Avocado, refried beans on the side, or even shredded chicken are fantastic additions to make your chilaquiles a complete meal.
article:
Unlock the vibrant flavors of Mexico right in your own kitchen with a dish that’s as comforting as it is exhilarating: Chilaquiles. While the concept of crispy tortillas drenched in sauce might sound simple, it's the rich, spicy, and utterly satisfying Chilaquiles Red Salsa that truly elevates this beloved Mexican breakfast to an art form. Forget bland mornings; preparing this authentic salsa from scratch promises a culinary adventure that will transform your breakfast routine. Get ready to dive into the heart of Mexican cuisine and master the secret to perfect Chilaquiles.

## What is Chilaquiles Red Salsa?

Chilaquiles, pronounced chee-lah-KEE-lehs, is a traditional Mexican breakfast dish consisting of lightly fried corn tortilla pieces (totopos) simmered in a flavorful salsa, typically red or green. It's often topped with crumbled cheese, sour cream, onion, cilantro, and sometimes a fried egg or shredded chicken. The star of the show, however, is undoubtedly the salsa itself. Chilaquiles Red Salsa is known for its deep, robust, and often spicy character, deriving its color and complexity from ripe tomatoes and dried red chiles.

Unlike a fresh pico de gallo or a mild table salsa, Chilaquiles Red Salsa is cooked down to develop a profound depth of flavor. It’s thicker, richer, and designed to coat and soften the tortilla chips just enough, without making them soggy too quickly. The heat level can range from pleasantly warm to intensely fiery, depending on the chiles used and personal preference. This salsa is not just a condiment; it’s the very soul of the dish, providing the tangy, savory, and spicy notes that make Chilaquiles an irresistible Mexican breakfast staple.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting an authentic Chilaquiles Red Salsa requires a few key components. The beauty lies in the quality and freshness of these ingredients.

For the Salsa:
*   6-8 medium Roma tomatoes, ripe
*   4-6 dried guajillo chiles, stems and seeds removed
*   2-3 dried árbol chiles (optional, for extra heat), stems and seeds removed
*   1/4 white onion, roughly chopped
*   2-3 cloves garlic, peeled
*   3-4 cups chicken broth (or vegetable broth for a vegetarian option)
*   2 tablespoons vegetable oil (or canola oil)
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cumin (optional, but adds depth)
*   Salt to taste
*   Freshly ground black pepper to taste

For Serving (Typical Chilaquiles Assembly):
*   1 bag (12-14 oz) good quality corn tortilla chips (or homemade totopos)
*   1/2 cup crumbled queso fresco or cotija cheese
*   1/4 cup Mexican crema or sour cream
*   1/4 red onion, thinly sliced
*   1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
*   Optional: Fried eggs, shredded cooked chicken, avocado slices

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Chilaquiles Red Salsa from scratch is a rewarding process that builds layers of flavor.

1.  **Prepare the Chiles:** Heat a dry skillet or comal over medium heat. Toast the dried guajillo and árbol chiles for 1-2 minutes per side, pressing them down with tongs. Be careful not to burn them, as this will make them bitter. They should become fragrant and slightly pliable. Once toasted, immediately transfer them to a heatproof bowl and cover with very hot water. Let them soak for 20-30 minutes until softened.
2.  **Roast the Vegetables:** While the chiles are soaking, preheat your broiler or oven to 400°F (200°C). Place the tomatoes, roughly chopped white onion, and peeled garlic cloves on a baking sheet. Broil for about 5-7 minutes, or roast for 15-20 minutes, turning halfway, until the tomatoes are slightly charred and softened, and the onion and garlic are fragrant. Alternatively, you can boil them in water until soft.
3.  **Blend the Salsa:** Once the chiles are soft, drain them, reserving about 1/2 cup of the soaking liquid. Transfer the softened chiles, roasted tomatoes, onion, and garlic to a blender. Add 3 cups of chicken broth, the ground cumin (if using), and a pinch of salt and pepper. Blend until completely smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add a little more broth or the reserved chile soaking liquid until it reaches your desired consistency.
4.  **Strain the Salsa (Optional but Recommended):** For an extra smooth Chilaquiles Red Salsa, pour the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, pressing down on the solids with the back of a spoon to extract all the liquid. Discard the solids. This step removes any remaining chile skins or seeds, resulting in a silkier salsa.
5.  **Simmer the Salsa:** Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large saucepan or Dutch oven over medium heat. Once the oil is hot, carefully pour in the strained salsa. Be cautious as it may splatter. Bring the salsa to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low and continue to cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally. This simmering process allows the flavors to meld and deepen, and the salsa will thicken slightly. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving truly spectacular Chilaquiles Red Salsa involves a few insider tricks:

*   **Don't Burn the Chiles:** Toasting dried chiles awakens their flavor, but burning them will make your salsa bitter. Keep a close eye on them and remove them from the heat as soon as they become fragrant.
*   **Roast for Depth:** While boiling the tomatoes, onion, and garlic works, roasting them adds a beautiful smoky depth and sweetness that elevates the salsa’s overall flavor profile.
*   **Strain for Smoothness:** While optional, straining the salsa creates a velvety smooth texture that is characteristic of restaurant-quality chilaquiles. If you prefer a rustic salsa, you can skip this step.
*   **Simmering is Key:** Don't rush the simmering process. Allowing the Chilaquiles Red Salsa to cook gently for at least 15-20 minutes allows all the flavors to meld beautifully and the salsa to thicken slightly, ensuring it coats the chips perfectly.
*   **Adjust Spice to Your Liking:** The combination of guajillo and árbol chiles offers a balanced heat. For a milder salsa, omit the árbol chiles. For more heat, add an extra árbol chile or a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Make Ahead:** Chilaquiles Red Salsa can be made a day or two in advance and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator. In fact, the flavors often deepen overnight!

## Serving Suggestions

Once your incredible Chilaquiles Red Salsa is ready, it's time to assemble the ultimate Mexican breakfast.

1.  **Warm the Chips:** Briefly warm your tortilla chips in the oven or microwave to ensure they are crisp and ready to absorb the delicious salsa.
2.  **Assemble and Serve Immediately:** In a large bowl or directly on individual plates, place a generous portion of warm tortilla chips. Ladle the hot Chilaquiles Red Salsa generously over the chips, ensuring most are coated.
3.  **Garnish Galore:** Immediately top with crumbled queso fresco or cotija cheese, a drizzle of Mexican crema or sour cream, thinly sliced red onion, and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro.
4.  **Protein Power-Up:** For a heartier meal, add a freshly fried egg (sunny-side up or over-easy is traditional), shredded cooked chicken, or even a side of refried beans. Avocado slices also make a fantastic addition, adding a creamy contrast.
5.  **Enjoy!** Chilaquiles are best enjoyed immediately, as the chips will soften over time.

## Variations and Substitutions

The beauty of Chilaquiles Red Salsa is its adaptability. Feel free to experiment and make it your own!

*   **Chile Choices:** Experiment with different dried red chiles. Ancho chiles offer a richer, smokier flavor with less heat, while cascabel chiles provide a nutty, slightly smoky taste. For a super spicy kick, a small amount of chile de arbol or even a habanero can be added.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Easily make this salsa vegetarian by using vegetable broth instead of chicken broth. For a fully vegan dish, omit the cheese and crema, or use plant-based alternatives.
*   **Herbal Notes:** For an authentic touch, add a sprig of epazote to the salsa as it simmers. This herb is commonly used in Mexican cuisine and imparts a unique, earthy flavor.
*   **Tomato Alternatives:** If fresh Roma tomatoes aren't available, good quality canned whole peeled tomatoes (drained) can be used as a substitute, though the flavor profile will be slightly different.
*   **Frying the Chips:** While store-bought chips are convenient, making your own totopos by frying corn tortillas cut into triangles until crispy adds an extra layer of freshness and control over the oil.

Mastering Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a culinary achievement that brings the soul of Mexican breakfast to your table. This vibrant, flavorful, and customizable dish is perfect for weekend brunches, special occasions, or simply whenever you crave a taste of authentic Mexico. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be transported to a world of exquisite flavors with every delectable bite of Chilaquiles!
